questions.text: Who is the author of the story 'The Rattrap'?
questions.explanation: Selma Lagerlöf is the Swedish author who wrote 'The Rattrap'.

questions.text: What is the central metaphor used by the protagonist in the story?
options: The world is a giant rattrap
questions.explanation: The peddler thinks that the whole world is a big rattrap that offers baits to people.

questions.text: What did the peddler sell to make a living?
questions.explanation: He made and sold small wire rattraps from material he begged for.

questions.text: Why did the peddler have to resort to both begging and petty thievery?
options: His business was not profitable enough
questions.explanation: Selling rattraps was not enough to keep body and soul together, so he had to beg and steal.

questions.text: How did the old crofter treat the peddler when he asked for shelter?
questions.explanation: The crofter was lonely and happy to have someone to talk to, so he welcomed the peddler warmly.

questions.text: What did the crofter show the peddler to prove his earning?
questions.explanation: He showed the peddler thirty kronor he had earned by selling milk to the creamery.

questions.text: What did the peddler do after leaving the crofter's house the next morning?
options: He returned and stole the thirty kronor
questions.explanation: He returned, smashed the window pane, and stole the money from the leather pouch.

questions.text: Why did the peddler decide to walk through the forest instead of the public highway?
options: He was afraid of being caught with the stolen money
questions.explanation: He didn't dare continue on the public highway because of the money he had stolen.

questions.text: What happened to the peddler in the forest?
options: He got lost and felt he was in a rattrap himself
He was attacked by wild animals
questions.explanation: The paths twisted back and forth; he realized he was walking in circles and felt trapped by his own 'bait'.

questions.text: Where did the peddler find shelter while he was in the forest?
questions.explanation: He heard the thumping of hammers and followed the sound to the Ramsjö Ironworks.

questions.text: Who did the ironmaster mistake the peddler for?
options: An old regimental comrade, Nils Olof
questions.explanation: The ironmaster mistook him for Captain von Stahle, a former comrade from the regiment.

questions.text: Why did the peddler decline the ironmaster's invitation to his manor?
options: He was afraid of the police
questions.explanation: Going to the manor with stolen money would be like throwing himself into the lion's den.

questions.text: Who finally persuaded the peddler to come to the manor house?
questions.explanation: Edla, the ironmaster's daughter, came to the mill and showed great compassion and kindness.

questions.text: What was the name of the ironmaster's daughter?
questions.explanation: Edla Willmansson is the daughter who brings about a change in the peddler.

questions.text: What did Edla notice about the peddler when she first met him?
options: He looked either afraid or like a thief
questions.explanation: She thought he looked as if he had stolen something or escaped from jail.

questions.text: When did the ironmaster realize his mistake about the peddler's identity?
options: In broad daylight after the peddler was groomed
questions.explanation: The next morning, after a bath and a shave, the ironmaster saw him clearly and realized he was not Nils Olof.

questions.text: What did the ironmaster want to do after realizing the peddler was not his friend?
questions.explanation: He was angry and wanted to hand the peddler over to the police.

questions.text: How did Edla react when her father wanted to kick the peddler out?
options: She insisted they should let him stay for Christmas Eve
questions.explanation: She argued that they had invited him and promised him a day of peace.

questions.text: What Christmas gift did the ironmaster give the peddler?
questions.explanation: The suit he was wearing was a gift, and he was told he could keep it.

questions.text: What did the ironmaster and Edla learn at the church on Christmas morning?
options: That the peddler was a king
That a crofter had been robbed by a man selling rattraps
That the mill was on fire
questions.explanation: They heard that an old crofter had been robbed of his thirty kronor by a peddler.

questions.text: What did Edla find when she returned from church and opened the peddler's gift?
options: A small rattrap with the 30 kronor and a note
questions.explanation: The peddler left a small rattrap, the stolen money, and a beautiful letter.

questions.text: How did the peddler sign the letter he left for Edla?
questions.explanation: He signed as Captain von Stahle because Edla had treated him like a real captain, helping him change.

questions.text: The peddler's change of heart is an example of:
options: The power of kindness and compassion
questions.explanation: Edla's unconditional kindness and trust transformed the thief back into a gentleman.

questions.text: What does the rattrap symbolize in the story?
options: Human greed and the traps of material lures
questions.explanation: It symbolizes how the world lures humans with riches and joys, only to trap them.

questions.text: What was the name of the iron mill in the story?
questions.explanation: The setting for the latter half of the story is the Ramsjö Ironworks.

questions.text: Why was the crofter so talkative and generous with his confidence?
options: He was lonely and had no wife or child
questions.explanation: Living alone in his cottage, he craved company and shared his secrets easily.

questions.text: The ironmaster's hospitality was based on:
questions.explanation: He only invited the peddler because he thought he was an old friend.

questions.text: How much money did the peddler steal from the crofter?
questions.explanation: He stole three 10-kronor notes.

questions.text: The Rattrap is set amidst the mines of which country?
questions.explanation: The story is set in the iron ore rich areas of Sweden.

questions.text: What did the peddler compare the 'baits' of the world to?
options: Cheese and pork in a rattrap
questions.explanation: He compared riches and joys to cheese and pork used to lure rats.

questions.text: What was Edla's reaction when her father decided to call the sheriff?
questions.explanation: She interceded on his behalf, showing empathy for his miserable life.

questions.text: What did the peddler do for most of his time at the manor house?
questions.explanation: Having not felt safe for years, he spent most of the time sleeping.

questions.text: The letter to Edla showed that the peddler had:
options: Been truly reformed by her goodness
questions.explanation: The letter proves his internal transformation and desire to be worthy of her kindness.

questions.text: What is the tone of the story?
questions.explanation: It has the elements of a moral fable or fairy tale with a redemptive ending.

questions.text: In the beginning, how did the peddler feel about the world?
options: He felt the world was unkind to him
questions.explanation: The world had never been kind to him, so he enjoyed thinking ill of it.

questions.text: What game did the crofter and the peddler play?
questions.explanation: They played a Swedish card game called Mjölis until bedtime.

questions.text: What does the story 'The Rattrap' primarily explore?
options: Basic human goodness that can be awakened through love
questions.explanation: The theme is that kindness can transform even a hardened person.

questions.text: How did the ironmaster try to help his 'friend' initially?
options: By inviting him home for Christmas
By giving him a job at the mill
questions.explanation: He insisted the peddler come home to celebrate Christmas with him and his daughter.

questions.text: What did the peddler request in his letter to Edla regarding the stolen money?
options: To give it back to the old crofter
questions.explanation: He asked her to return the money to the old man on the roadside.

questions.text: What was the peddler's state of mind when he 'signed' himself as Captain von Stahle?
options: He wanted to live up to the dignity Edla gave him
questions.explanation: By signing this way, he acknowledged that her treatment of him as a captain helped him act like one.
